item.title: China’s Energy Imports Plunge as War Chokes Hormuz Shipments
item.description: Chinese energy imports fell sharply in April, as the near-halt to shipments through the Strait of Hormuz choked a vital channel for crude oil and natural gas.

item.title: China Gold Output Falls as Investor Demand for Bars, Coins Jumps
item.description: China’s gold output declined in the first quarter of 2026, weighed down by safety inspections and production suspensions, according to data from the China Gold Association.

item.title: Chinese Export Growth Rebounds as War Fails to Curb Trade
item.description: China’s export growth rebounded more than expected despite disruptions to shipping caused by the war in Iran, as trade volumes swell due to an investment boom in artificial intelligence.

item.title: China’s Top Economic Planner Urges Stronger Coordination on AI
item.description: China’s leading economic planning agency called for stronger coordination and top-level planning in artificial intelligence development.

item.title: Colombia Inflation Ticks Up, Fueling Case For Renewed Rate Hikes
item.description: Colombian inflation sped up in April, moving further away from the central bank’s target and raising the likelihood it may need to resume interest rate hikes after an unexpected pause late last month.

item.title: Fed’s Waller Says Regional Feds Will Centralize Some Functions
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said the 12 regional Fed banks had agreed to a framework on how to standardize and centralize some functions, such as back-office operations.

item.title: Judge to Review Musk, SEC Settlement Deal in Twitter Stake Case
item.description: A federal judge is asking for more information about a proposed settlement between Elon Musk and the US Securities and Exchange Commission, after the billionaire agreed to pay $1.5 million to end a lawsuit alleging he waited too long to disclose his growing stake in Twitter.

item.title: Florida, Louisiana-Focused Insurer Safepoint Files for IPO
item.description: Safepoint Holdings Inc., an underwriter of specialty homeowners and commercial insurance, filed for an initial public offering, disclosing growing profit and revenue.

item.title: Pakistan to Receive $1.32 Billion as IMF Board Approves Tranches
item.description: Pakistan will receive about $1.32 billion after the International Monetary Fund Board approved loan tranches under existing facilities, boosting the country’s ability to shield its economy from increased global risks.

item.title: BlackRock Readies Launch of Two Tokenized Money-Market Funds
item.description: BlackRock Inc. is planning to launch two money-market funds built for investors who hold their cash in stablecoins, not bank accounts, a sign the world’s largest asset manager sees a durable customer base in the digital-dollar economy.

item.title: Nvidia Names Goldman Sachs Veteran Suzanne Nora Johnson to Board
item.description: Nvidia Corp. named former Goldman Sachs Group Inc. Vice Chairman Suzanne Nora Johnson as a director, adding a finance-industry and philanthropy veteran to the chipmaker’s board.

item.title: Honeywell-Backed Computing Firm Quantinuum Files for US IPO
item.description: Quantinuum Inc., a quantum computing company backed by Honeywell International Inc., filed for a US initial public offering, capitalizing on enthusiasm around quantum computing stocks.

item.title: Burry Buys MercadoLibre After Slump, Eyes 15% Returns
item.description: Michael Burry is buying shares of MercadoLibre Inc. after a slump in the Latin American e-commerce and fintech company’s stock.

item.title: Momentum Trade at New Extremes Is Drawing Wall Street Warnings
item.description: The momentum trade — buy what’s winning, sell what isn’t — was already running red-hot heading into this week. Now a pause in the Iran war, strong US jobs data and another sharp rally in AI chips just piled on more fuel.

item.title: Colombia Gives Priority to TGI’s LNG Project as Gas Dwindles
item.description: Transportadora de Gas Internacional’s planned liquefied natural gas import terminal in Colombia moved closer to execution after the government added it to a list of infrastructure priorities designed to address a growing shortfall in domestic gas supply.

item.title: Fed Calls Risks Tied to Private Credit Redemptions ‘Manageable’
item.description: The Federal Reserve said stability risks from further private credit redemption requests appear “limited and manageable,” after some of the biggest names in the market blocked investors from getting money out of their funds in recent months.

item.title: Applied Aerospace Files for IPO, Joining Pre-SpaceX Rush
item.description: Applied Aerospace & Defense Inc. filed for a US initial public offering, joining a recent rush of space and defense firms to public markets ahead of the potential listing of SpaceX later this year.

item.title: Dunkin’ Owner Inspire Brands Files Confidentially for US IPO
item.description: Inspire Brands Inc., the owner of fast-food chains Dunkin’, Arby’s and Jimmy John’s, filed confidentially for a US initial public offering.

item.title: Private School Charging $76,000 Offers Discount in Revival Plan
item.description: A Colorado private school has tapped the debt markets for $27 million to fund campus upgrades while offering discounts on tuition as part of a turnaround plan.

item.title: Blue Owl BDC Won Big New Investment by University of California
item.description: A $200 billion investment fund for the University of California system added almost 30 million shares of Blue Owl Technology Finance Corp. in the first quarter, in a sign of institutions seizing upon volatility in the private credit market.

item.title: Fed’s Goolsbee Says All Interest-Rate Options Are On The Table
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Austan Goolsbee said all options on interest rates are on the table right now.

item.title: AI Chipmaker Cerebras Is Said to Plan Raising IPO Price Range
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. is set to increase the price range of its initial public offering as soon as Monday, according to people familiar with the matter, as demand for the artificial intelligence chipmaker’s shares continues to build.

item.title: QVC Shareholders Claim Firm Giving Away Their Value to Creditors
item.description: Shareholders of bankrupt television shopping network QVC have accused the company of wrongly trying to give away hundreds of millions dollars worth of assets to other stakeholders as part of a proposal to cut debt and end the company’s insolvency case.

item.title: Google’s Isomorphic Labs to Raise Over $2 Billion in New Funding
item.description: Isomorphic Labs, an AI-powered drug discovery company spun out of Alphabet Inc.’s Google DeepMind, is in advanced discussions to raise more than $2 billion in a new round of funding, according to people familiar with the plan.

item.title: Musk, Altman Management Styles Come Under Fire at OpenAI Trial
item.description: Elon Musk and Sam Altman both took a beating over their leadership styles in court testimony this week as a jury waded further into their OpenAI feud.

item.title: NYC School District Hit by Malware Attack as Well as Canvas Hack
item.description: As the New York City Public School system grapples with the Canvas service hack affecting schools nationwide, one of its Manhattan campuses is contending with a separate incident.

item.title: KNDS IPO Wobbles Over Valuation as Peers Fall
item.description: KNDS NV’s plans for a summer initial public offering could be in doubt because of concerns about the tank maker’s valuation, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Milken Crowd Hates the Phrase ‘Semi-Liquid’ After Retail Exodus
item.description: ‘Semi-liquid is kind of a diabolical name’

item.title: German Banks Look to Offload Troubled Loan on LA Office Tower
item.description: German lenders are looking to sell a non-performing loan on the CalEdison Building in downtown Los Angeles, which has struggled to attract tenants.

item.title: Anthropic Inks $1.8 Billion Computing Deal With Akamai
item.description: Anthropic PBC has signed a $1.8 billion computing deal with cloud services provider Akamai Technologies Inc. to meet surging demand for its artificial intelligence software, according to people familiar with the matter.
